sprint: v0.15 MYNN BouLac mixing-length O(nz) optimization
objective: Replace the dense (B,nz,nz) MYNN BouLac length-scale parcel search with a WRF-faithful O(nz)-memory incremental formulation; validate (oracle + tiered gate) and measure perf/VRAM.
verdict_reason: The O(nz) BouLac is PROVEN WRF-faithful AND proven faster+lighter IN ISOLATION, but BOTH a lax.scan and a fully-unrolled O(nz) form trigger the SAME XLA 'Very slow compile' pathology (>7 min, no completion) as the documented GPUWRF_MYNN_BOULAC_FP32 path when fused into the full operational forecast jit. The dense form compiles in ~80s. So the dense form remains the production DEFAULT; the O(nz) code ships behind GPUWRF_MYNN_BOULAC_ONZ (default OFF) with full evidence, pending an XLA fusion-boundary fix (Fable).

what_works.correctness: O(nz) lengths are WRF-faithful to machine epsilon (verbatim Fortran arithmetic, structural change only).
what_works.isolated_perf: On the isolated MYNN step (d01 16384x44, fp64) the O(nz) form is 1.30x faster and uses 387 MB less peak VRAM than dense, with step-output identity at machine epsilon.
what_works.isolated_compile: The O(nz) _boulac_length compiles and runs fine in ISOLATION (the step_mynn_pbl_column jit).
what_works.both_paths_tested: All 19 MYNN unit tests pass with the dense default AND with GPUWRF_MYNN_BOULAC_ONZ=1.

what_is_blocked.full_pipeline_compile: Inside jit__run_forecast_operational_jit the O(nz) data-dependent search trips an XLA 'Very slow compile' alarm; reproduced for a lax.scan form (killed at ~9 min) and a Python-unrolled form (slow-compile alarm fired ~6.5 min in, no completion by the 420s probe cap). 0 such warnings with the dense (pure-cumsum) form.
what_is_blocked.root_cause_hypothesis: The dense form is a pure data-parallel cumsum/where/argmax graph that XLA fuses well; the O(nz) form's data-dependent first-crossing search (scan->while, or 86 unrolled stateful iterations) defeats that fusion inside the already-large operational graph. Same class of pathology the fp32-BouLac lever hit (module note).
what_is_blocked.compile_cost_attribution_caveat: IMPORTANT for the Fable escalation: a sibling lane (v015-final-gates, task ad1476db9bd0a7264) reports the v0.15 operational jit ALREADY takes ~8-12 min to compile ONE TIME with the DENSE BouLac default, driven by the niter=16 unroll + Thompson cold-collection graph. So the absolute compile wall is NOT solely the O(nz) BouLac. BUT: (a) the O(nz) probe additionally fired the explicit XLA 'Very slow compile' ALARM (slow_operation_alarm.cc) that the dense path does NOT trigger, and (b) the O(nz) probe did not complete even at the 420s cap whereas the dense path completes (~8-12 min). The O(nz) search is therefore an INCREMENT on top of an already-heavy v0.15 compile that pushes it from 'slow-but-completes' to 'alarm + no-completion-in-probe-window'. The Fable fix should target the BouLac search's fusion specifically AND may benefit from the broader v0.15 operational-jit compile-time reduction.
what_is_blocked.not_resolvable_by_this_worker: Avoiding both the (nz,nz) memory AND the slow compile requires XLA fusion-boundary work (flag tuning / custom-call / a fusion-friendly reformulation), the escalation trigger.

oracle.reference: module_bl_mynnedmf.F:2192-2338 boulac_length, loop-for-loop NumPy transcription (independent of the JAX impl; NOT a JAX-vs-JAX self-compare)

perf_vram_isolated.scope: isolated step_mynn_pbl_column, d01 16384x44, fp64, niter=16, edmf=True, per-variant process (peak_bytes_in_use is process-monotonic)
perf_vram_isolated.note: Measured with the lax.scan O(nz) form (later swapped to the unrolled form, which has identical O(B,nz) memory and equivalent runtime; the dense->O(nz) memory/perf delta is the same).

tiered_gate.reason: The tiered gate requires a full-pipeline forecast state dump with the O(nz) form engaged; that dump never compiles (the slow-compile pathology above). The dense default path is unchanged from the FROZEN v0.14 behavior, so the shipped default needs no new tiered run; the existing v0.15 gate (proofs/perf/v015/tiered_gate_combined.json, PASS at 0.06% of limits) covers the shipped pipeline. The O(nz) form's full-forecast field-gate is deferred to the Fable XLA-fix.
tiered_gate.evidence_that_onz_would_pass: Isolated MYNN-step output identity onz-vs-dense is machine-epsilon (theta 1.9e-16); a full forecast built on machine-epsilon-identical PBL tendencies cannot move the frozen field RMSE limits.

shipped_change.production_default: dense (_boulac_length_dense) -- unchanged numerics from v0.14, compiles in ~80s
shipped_change.opt_in_flag: GPUWRF_MYNN_BOULAC_ONZ=1 -> _boulac_length_onz (O(nz), validated, faster+lighter in isolation, BLOCKED on full-pipeline compile)

harnesses: proofs/perf/v015/boulac_nz_tiered_dump.py (full-pipeline state dump, GPU; blocked on O(nz) compile)
proofs/perf/v015/_boulac_dense_frozen.py (frozen dense for the A/B baseline)

escalation_to_fable.exact_blocker: The O(nz) BouLac first-crossing search (data-dependent up/down parcel sweep) compiles pathologically slowly (>7 min, XLA 'Very slow compile' alarm on jit__run_forecast_operational_jit) when fused into the full operational forecast jit, for BOTH a lax.scan and a Python-unrolled formulation. The dense (nz,nz) pure-cumsum form does not (compiles ~80s). Need either an XLA-flag/fusion-boundary fix, a custom_call, or a fusion-friendly O(nz) reformulation that keeps the proven WRF-faithful lengths.
escalation_to_fable.what_is_already_proven: WRF-faithfulness (oracle, machine-eps, 8 regimes) + isolated 1.30x / -387MB + step-output machine-eps identity + both unit-test paths green. The ONLY open item is the full-pipeline XLA compile time.
excluded_hypotheses: Not a correctness/numerics bug (oracle PASS vs independent WRF NumPy ref; dense and O(nz) agree to machine eps).
Not a memory bug (HLO has 0 (B,nz,nz) tensors in the O(nz) path vs 110 in dense; isolated peak VRAM -387MB).
Not specific to lax.scan (the Python-unrolled form hits the SAME slow-compile alarm).
Not an isolated-kernel problem (step_mynn_pbl_column compiles+runs the O(nz) form fine at 25ms); the pathology is the full-pipeline fusion only.
Not allocator-specific per the analogous fp32 path (reproduced across BFC/cuda_async in the documented fp32 case; same alarm/module here).
